name: Z-Stack 3.x Coordinator (CC2652/CC1352)
label: Z-Stack 3.x Coordinator/Router — TI CC2652/CC1352
description: Koenkk/Z-Stack-firmware — Zigbee 3.x coordinator (and router) firmware for TI CC2652/CC1352 dongles (Sonoff ZBDongle-E, CC2652R7, CC2652RB, SLZB/ZigStar). A coordinator is an active TX radio performing legitimate Zigbee protocol operation -> no deauth/jam/injection. CC flashes the vendor image over the TI serial ROM bootloader.
note: Every release asset is a .zip wrapping ONE .hex (verified: CC2652R7_coordinator_20250321.zip -> one 506630 B .hex). Resolver MUST unzip before flashing (no raw .hex in-tree). BSL entry differs per dongle (Sonoff vs ZigStar vs bare LaunchPad) -> per-board bootloader-trigger param; LaunchPads are XDS110/UniFlash-only (out of scope). app_offset (full-image mass-erase, likely 0x0) + BSL baud kept verify: until a real CC2652 flash. Second consumer of the cc2538_bsl backend. danger '' -- a coordinator is a legit-protocol TX radio, not an attack tool. Compiled binary carries TI's SimpleLink SDK binary license -- flag before any LxveLabs mirror.
